name: Chained Exploit Followed by Suspicious Events on Third Host
notes:
This rule triggers when an exploit or attack type activity is observed on a system and a post exploit event is seen from that host within 15 minutes. 

Note: This rule is disabled by default because it is intended as an alternative to the rule Chained Exploit Followed by Suspicious Events that will ignore events with the same Source and Destination IP.
bb_references: BB:CategoryDefinition: Post Exploit Account Activity in order from the same destination IP from the previous sequence
conditions_text: when Source IP not equals Destination IP

when a subset of at least 1 of these BB:CategoryDefinition: Exploits Backdoors and Trojans, in order, with the same source IP followed by a subset of at least 1 of these BB:CategoryDefinition: Post Exploit Account Activity in order from the same destination IP from the previous sequence, within 15 minutes
thresholds: when a subset of at least 1 of these BB:CategoryDefinition: Exploits Backdoors and Trojans, in order, with the same source IP followed by a subset of at least 1 of these BB:CategoryDefinition: Post Exploit Account Activity in order from the same destination IP from the previous sequence, within 15 minutes
TD_list: APPLY Chained Exploit Followed by Suspicious Events on Third Host on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when Source IP not equals Destination IP

AND when a subset of at least 1 of these BB:CategoryDefinition: Exploits Backdoors and Trojans, in order, with the same source IP followed by a subset of at least 1 of these BB:CategoryDefinition: Post Exploit Account Activity in order from the same destination IP from the previous sequence, within 15 minutes
RED_list: Event name: Exploit Followed by Suspicious Activity on Different Hosts - Chained

Event description: An exploit or attack type activity detected from a source IP followed by suspicious host activity from the destination to a different destination within 15 minutes.  This could indicate a compromised host.  To find the original attacking host search for the source of this event as a target of an exploit within the previous 15 minutes.
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Source IP
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
